summary: The current app writes HKCU\Software\Microsoft\GameBar\AutoGameModeEnabled = 1. Guest-side Procmon captures on 2026-03-20 against the interactive Administrator profile confirmed SystemSettings.exe queries AutoGameModeEnabled with Data:1 and Data:0 in separate reversible captures. That validates the live Game Mode registry mapping on this build even though Microsoft did not publish the raw registry key in the feature documentation captured here.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: gamemode_admin_probe.txt: "7:52:45.6879293 PM","SystemSettings.exe","5512","RegQueryValue","HKU\S-1-5-21-3538642439-2106388720-149684979-500\Software\Microsoft\GameBar\AutoGameModeEnabled","SUCCESS","Type: REG_DWORD, Length: 4, Data: 1". gamemode_admin_zero_probe.txt: "7:54:41.9130012 PM","SystemSettings.exe","5512","RegQueryValue","HKU\S-1-5-21-3538642439-2106388720-149684979-500\Software\Microsoft\GameBar\AutoGameModeEnabled","SUCCESS","Type: REG_DWORD, Length: 4, Data: 0".
validation_proof.notes: The interactive Administrator profile was probed through the guest. The value was set to 1 and then to 0 in separate reversible captures, and SystemSettings.exe read both states. The value was restored to 1 after the 0-state probe.

setting.name: Game Mode
setting.casual_explanation: Game Mode is Windows' built-in gaming feature that tries to reduce interruptions and prioritize the current game. Most users either leave it on or benchmark it for a specific game.
setting.why_people_change_it: People enable it when they want Windows to stay focused on a running game and reduce background interruptions like restart notifications.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Game Mode is a real Windows feature, but its exact impact can vary by game and system. The feature is official, and the registry mapping is now runtime-observed on this build even though Microsoft did not publish the raw key in the captured feature documentation.
setting.professional_notes: The repo research notes say toggling Game Mode in Settings switches HKCU\Software\Microsoft\GameBar\AutoGameModeEnabled and that a missing value behaves like 1. Microsoft officially documents the Game Mode feature and its purpose. Guest-side Procmon on 2026-03-20 confirmed that the interactive Administrator profile's SystemSettings.exe reads AutoGameModeEnabled as a live setting on this build.

allowed_values.label: Game Mode disabled
allowed_values.meaning: Observed runtime state for AutoGameModeEnabled on this build. Guest-side Procmon captured SystemSettings.exe querying the Administrator hive with Data:0 in a reversible capture.

allowed_values.label: Game Mode enabled
allowed_values.meaning: Observed runtime state for AutoGameModeEnabled on this build. Guest-side Procmon captured SystemSettings.exe querying the Administrator hive with Data:1 in a reversible capture.

app_current_implementation.notes: The app targets an official Windows gaming feature, and the guest-side Procmon capture on 2026-03-20 confirmed the live Game Mode registry read path on the interactive Administrator profile.

windows_defaults.label: Conservative baseline
windows_defaults.applies_to: Systems where this dataset has not yet validated AutoGameModeEnabled from a primary Microsoft source

states.rationale: Do not publish a validated registry baseline until the exact Game Mode mapping is backed by a primary Microsoft source.

windows_defaults.notes: This keeps the feature documented without overstating confidence in the registry path.

People benchmarking games on this build
avoid_for: Users who prefer Game Mode off

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want Windows Game Mode enabled for the current user.
tradeoffs: Game Mode stays active for the current profile.
The live registry read path is now validated on this build.

recommended_profiles.label: Current app profile

states.rationale: Observed app write used as the current Game Mode enabled profile.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You are documenting what the app currently writes for Game Mode.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You need a different Game Mode state than the app writes.
tradeoffs: The current app behavior is captured.
The exact registry semantics are validated by guest-side Procmon reads on the interactive Administrator profile.
recommended_profiles.notes: Validated by guest-side Procmon reads on the interactive Administrator profile.

evidence.title: Xbox Support: Use Game Mode while gaming on your Windows device
evidence.summary: Microsoft officially documents the Game Mode feature and its user-facing behavior, confirming that Game Mode is a real Windows control surface even though this page does not publish the raw registry key.

evidence.title: Procmon capture - Game Mode AutoGameModeEnabled reads on Administrator profile
evidence.summary: Guest-side Procmon captures against the interactive Administrator hive showed SystemSettings.exe querying HKU\S-1-5-21-3538642439-2106388720-149684979-500\Software\Microsoft\GameBar\AutoGameModeEnabled with Data:1 and with Data:0 in separate reversible captures. The value was restored to 1 after the 0-state probe.

evidence.title: Repo system research notes for Game Mode
evidence.summary: The repo documents AutoGameModeEnabled under HKCU\Software\Microsoft\GameBar and notes that toggling Game Mode in Settings appears to switch this value.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes AutoGameModeEnabled = 1 for the current tweak.

decision.why: Guest-side Procmon captures against the interactive Administrator profile confirmed that SystemSettings.exe reads AutoGameModeEnabled as a live registry setting on this build. The app's current write matches the observed 1-state, and the 0-state was also observed in a reversible capture.
